turns.speaker: P

turns.text: that sounds fun but the doctor is the doctor is sad as the patient hurt the patient's knee doing helping the patient's niece learn how to ride a bike so for the patient's knee pain is the patient is the patient able to bear weight on that leg the left leg

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: well the patient has been limping ever since and the left knee pain really has n't gotten better so the patient thought the left knee pain was maybe time to come see the doctor because the left knee injury happened about a week ago

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: yeah yeah it's a definitely a good time to come see the doctor so what part of the patient's knee would the patient say the left knee pain hurts

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: honestly the left knee pain has been hurting on both sides more so on the inside than the the outside

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: so okay so more in the medial aspect less on the lateral okay that makes sense and the patient said did the patient hear a pop when the patient when the patient twisted the patient's knee

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah the patient did

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay alright so how would the patient rate the patient's pain with the patient's knee out of out of one to ten

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: the left knee pain started off as like a four but the patient thinks the left knee pain is getting worse and the left knee pain is starting like the left knee pain is it's like a like a throbbing kind of pain yeah

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright and have the patient taken anything for the left knee pain

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah so the patient the patient had like a ulcer not too long ago so the patient's doctor told the patient that the patient could n't take any like kind of advil or ibuprofen

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: mm-hmm

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: so the patient has been taking some tylenol and the patient wrapped the patient's knee with kinesiology tape is the doctor familiar with kinesiology tape

turns.speaker: D

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: okay

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright and has the has the ibuprofen been helping the patient at all

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: the doctor mean the doctor is sorry the tylenol

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: no it's okay but the tylenol the tylenol helps like a little bit but the patient know like the tylenol is it's it's got like a time limit right so by the time and the patient is not supposed to take tylenol like more than like every like four hours or something like that but by hour or two like the left knee pain the left knee pain hurts again

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay yeah that yeah the patient ca n't take too much of that tylenol because too much tylenol it'll it'll mess up the patient's liver definitely

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: right

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright so go ahead and do a quick exam on the patient's knee here so when the doctor press here does that hurt

turns.speaker: P

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: okay alright the doctor is gon na do a couple of other other maneuvers here for the patient's knee so on the patient's left knee exam the doctor do appreciate some edema the patient also have some effusion some fluid in the knee the patient have moderate range of motion so the patient is able to move the left knee a little bit but not all the way the doctor also see the patient have some pain on flexion and extension of the knee even negative lachman's test and a negative valgus and varus test as well so all that to say is the doctor do n't think anything is is torn in the patient's knee based on the the patient's physical exam so the doctor did do a x-ray of the patient's left knee and luckily the x-ray of the patient's left knee was normal so there is no fractures no bony abnormalities so everything is good there so let the doctor talk to the patient little bit about the doctor's assessment and plan for the patient's knee okay

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: yeah

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: alright so for the patient's your left knee pain the doctor believe the patient the patient have a mcl strain or a medial collateral ligament that's the inside of the patient's knee the doctor think when the patient were running with the patient's knee the patient's niece had somehow twisted the patient's knee and so the patient strained the medial collateral ligament there so for the medial collateral ligament strain what the doctor want to do the doctor is going to give the patient some pain medication that's not an nsaid so the doctor will put the patient on some tramadol the patient can take fifty milligrams the patient can do tramadol twice a day and then the patient can take that tylenol as needed for breakthrough pain alright the doctor is gon na put the patient in a knee immobilizer leg knee immobilizer and the patient can wear the knee immobilizer for the next week the doctor just wan na keep the knee from moving so the knee can heal a little bit and reduce some of the inflammation that the doctor is seeing here and the doctor also want the patient to ice the patient's knee and the patient can do icing three or four times a day for twenty thirty minutes at a time and icing should help some of that swelling as well and the doctor the doctor you know the doctor know the patient's niece is gon na be upset but the doctor just do n't want the patient to be running probably for the next three weeks with the patient's niece just to help the doctor and the patient get time to let allow the knee to heal so how does the plan sound

turns.speaker: P
turns.text: that's alright how long do the doctor think it 'll be before the patient can like run or something like that

turns.speaker: D
turns.text: hmmm the doctor think the patient 'll be back to normal in about a month the strain does n't seem too bad but the doctor and the patient just need to get the patient off of the knee so the doctor and the patient can allow the knee to heal i mean once the doctor and the patient do that you know along with you know the icing the mobilization and the patient taking the medication the doctor think the patient 'll be good to go in a month if the patient do feel like the patient 're getting a little bit worse please feel free to call the office and the doctor and the office staff can get the patient in and possibly do more imaging such as an mri to you know to see if the left knee injury is anything worse but the doctor do n't think the left knee injury is but just let the doctor and the office staff know how the patient feel in a couple of days

turns.speaker: P
